title: Embodied Cognition Augmented End2End Autonomous Driving (E³AD / E-Cubed-AD)

repos.name: AIR-DISCOVER__E-cubed-AD (main branch)
repos.evidence: README.md is 14 bytes: '# Embodied Cognition Augmented End2End Autonomous Driving / $$E^3AD$$: E-Cubed-AD / code coming soon'. Only README.md is tracked on the default main branch; no training/eval/method code. This is the shallow snapshot the ORIGINAL audit cloned and wrongly read as 'no code'.

repos.name: AIR-DISCOVER__E-cubed-AD__E-VAD (non-default E-VAD branch)
repos.evidence: git remote = https://github.com/AIR-DISCOVER/E-cubed-AD (the EXACT URL stated in the paper abstract, p.1), owner org AIR-DISCOVER = the authors' lab (Tsinghua AIR). 364 .py files. Stage-1 'Driving-Thinking Model': projects/eeg_vedio/src/models/contrastive_model.py (CLIP-style InfoNCE), eeg_encoder/labram.py (the LaBraM EEG large model cited [13]), video_encoder/video_encoder.py (Video Swin). Stage-2: projects/mmdet3d_plugin/EAD/EAD.py registers class EAD(MVXTwoStageDetector) docstring 'EAD model. Driving_think based VAD', importing the frozen VideoEncoder 'driving_thinking model' — exactly the paper's embodied-cognition-augmented E2E planner. Open-loop nuScenes metrics in EAD/planner/metric_stp3.py. This IS the paper's own core implementation.

rationale: The paper's own core (both stages of E³AD) is present on the AIR-DISCOVER/E-cubed-AD E-VAD branch, owned by the authors and matching the code URL printed in the abstract. The original audit cloned only the shallow default main branch (a 14-byte 'code coming soon' README) and emitted four high-severity 'no code / no training / no eval / no ablation' findings — i.e. it asserted no code exists while the author core was in fact published on a non-default branch. This is the classic stale-no-code failure mode. The second clone (E-VAD) is the author core; the main-branch clone is a stub, not a different paper / baseline / dependency, so this is stale_no_code rather than contamination.
audit_impact: ALREADY CORRECTED by the in-place re-audit dated 2026-06-03. audit.md and findings_verified.json fetched the E-VAD branch and marked the four absence findings (repo-only-readme-placeholder, training-code-absent, eval-code-absent-openloop-closedloop, ablation-code-absent) as supplement_verdict='false_positive' (code exists), excluding them from severity totals. The surviving findings (F01 eeg-video-dataset-absent, F02 eeg-preprocessing-absent, F03 weights-and-deps narrowed, F05 closed-loop CARLA harness, F06 multi-baseline, F07 injection frameworks 1-2, F08 stage1-not-runnable, F09 hyperparams, F10 learnable-temperature) are genuine code-vs-claim gaps verified against the real E-VAD code, not 'no code' claims. No high-severity 'missing code' finding remains wrongly written against a non-core repo.
recommended_action: No further flip needed: the re-audit already reclassified the four 'no code' findings as false positives and re-pointed the audit at the authors' core implementation on the E-VAD branch. Keep the paper as source-present/core-present. Optionally tighten the clone tooling to fetch non-default branches so the original stale-no-code error does not recur for other papers; the surviving E-VAD findings should be retained as-is.
